subject: Sociology
title: Gender and Crime
description: A fast breakdown of Gender and Crime for builders.

hook.text: Men commit about 80% of violent crime worldwide. That's the biggest pattern in criminology. Why?

buildup.text: Sociologists explain the gender gap through masculinity norms: toughness, risk-taking, and dominance.

discovery.text: Boys are socialized to express frustration through aggression. Girls learn to internalize it as anxiety instead.

twist.text: Women's crime is rising — not because women changed, but because policing and reporting patterns shifted.

climax.text: Gender is the strongest predictor of criminal behavior. Understanding masculinity is key to understanding crime.

punchline.text: Crime has a gender — and it's taught.

quiz.question: Why do men commit more violent crime according to sociologists?
options: Masculinity norms that encourage toughness and risk-taking
Women commit just as much crime
